instruction: Soil test report for Cuttack, Kharif season. Readings: N=120 kg/ha, P=60 kg/ha, K=40 kg/ha, pH=6.5. Recommend the best crop and fertilizer with dosage.
response: Recommended crop: Rice. Apply UREA + DAP at 120+60 kg/ha.

instruction: Soil test report for Puri, Kharif season. Readings: N=100 kg/ha, P=50 kg/ha, K=50 kg/ha, pH=6.8. Recommend the best crop and fertilizer with dosage.
response: Recommended crop: Paddy. Apply UREA + SSP at 100+80 kg/ha.

instruction: Soil test report for Ganjam, Kharif season. Readings: N=80 kg/ha, P=40 kg/ha, K=40 kg/ha, pH=7.0. Recommend the best crop and fertilizer with dosage.
response: Recommended crop: Maize. Apply NPK 10:26:26 at 150 kg/ha.

instruction: Soil test report for Koraput, Kharif season. Readings: N=60 kg/ha, P=30 kg/ha, K=30 kg/ha, pH=5.8. Recommend the best crop and fertilizer with dosage.
response: Recommended crop: Finger Millet. Apply UREA + MOP at 80+40 kg/ha.

instruction: Soil test report for Balasore, Kharif season. Readings: N=40 kg/ha, P=60 kg/ha, K=20 kg/ha, pH=6.2. Recommend the best crop and fertilizer with dosage.
response: Recommended crop: Groundnut. Apply DAP + GYPSUM at 60+200 kg/ha.

instruction: Soil test report for Khordha, Kharif season. Readings: N=90 kg/ha, P=70 kg/ha, K=80 kg/ha, pH=6.4. Recommend the best crop and fertilizer with dosage.
response: Recommended crop: Tomato. Apply NPK 19:19:19 at 200 kg/ha.

instruction: Soil test report for Sambalpur, Kharif season. Readings: N=110 kg/ha, P=55 kg/ha, K=35 kg/ha, pH=7.2. Recommend the best crop and fertilizer with dosage.
response: Recommended crop: Wheat. Apply UREA + DAP at 110+55 kg/ha.

instruction: Soil test for Cuttack, Kharif season. Values: N=45.0, P=28.0, K=28.0, pH=6.3, rainfall=1200.0, temp=30.0. Recommend crop and fertilizer.
response:
Recommended crop(s): Rice, Paddy, Wheat, Maize, Groundnut, Jute, Tomato, Finger Millet. Fertilizers to apply: UREA, DAP, MOP, NPK, SSP, GYPSUM, ZINC SULPHATE, FYM. Details: Odisha Agriculture Department — Soil Health &
Crop Recommendation Report
In Cuttack district during Kharif season, soils with nitrogen levels of 45 kg/ha, phosphorus of 28
kg/ha, potassium of 38 kg/ha, and pH of 6.3 are best suited for paddy cultivation. Farmers are
advised to apply Urea at 120 kg/ha in split doses and DAP at 60 kg/ha as basal dose. Organic
carbon content of 0.52% indicates low fertility; FYM application is recommended at 5 tonnes/ha.
